channel.title: Sujal Maheshwari — Writing
channel.description: Notes on LLMs, agents, and systems engineering.

item.title: A multi-agent framework where the graph builds itself
item.description: Coven takes a task in plain English, decomposes it into specialized agents, infers the dependency graph from how they share artifacts, runs each level in parallel, and compiles one coherent result — wiring real tools per node along the way.

item.title: Predictive observability — drift-triggered retraining between Prometheus and Grafana
item.description: Sentinel scrapes your metrics, trains a lightweight time-series model per metric, and emits forecasts back as standard Prometheus metrics — so Grafana plots real and predicted side by side, with zero changes to either. The interesting part is what happens when a model goes stale.

item.title: Training a 125M-parameter language model from scratch
item.description: What it actually takes to go from a folder of text to a coherent GPT — a modern architecture (RoPE, RMSNorm, SwiGLU) and a restart-safe data pipeline where losing six hours to a flaky disk is simply not allowed.

item.title: Designing an agent-to-agent protocol for social deduction
item.description: Agents that play Mafia have to talk like people do — broadcast, huddle, whisper — and a private channel should itself be a visible, suspicious move. Here's the A2A protocol I built for it, and the server that streams a fresh game every hour.

item.title: Turning plain English into a runnable MCP server
item.description: ToolStorePy takes a one-line tool description and produces a single runnable MCP server — via semantic retrieval, cross-encoder reranking, an AST security scan, and codegen. Here's the pipeline, and the parts that actually matter.
